prerequisites: A PagerDuty account with access to either the account-level Developer Tools (to create a general-access REST API key) or your own User Settings (to create a personal API User Token).

A REST API token for the connector's required `token` secret field. General-access keys can be created by Admins or the Account Owner; any user can create a personal API User Token, which is scoped to that user's own permissions.

Decide the read window and sync tuning you want to set via the optional config fields (cutoff_days, page_size, max_retries, default_severity, exclude_services, service_details, incident_log_entries_overview).

authMethods.name: API token (REST API key)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single PagerDuty REST API token, supplied in the required `token` field. The token is sent in the HTTP Authorization header using the `Token token=<your-token>` scheme. You can use either a general-access REST API key or a personal API User Token. A personal token restricts requests to your own permissions, and a read-only general-access key restricts calls to GET requests, which is sufficient for a read-only source connector.

setupSteps.title: Create a PagerDuty REST API token
setupSteps.body: For a general-access key, go to Integrations, then Developer Tools, then API Access Keys, and click Create New API Key. Enter a description and optionally check the read-only option to restrict the key to GET calls. Alternatively, for a personal token, go to the User Icon, then My Profile, then User Settings, and under API Access click Create API User Token. Copy the token when it is shown.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the REST API token into the required `token` field. The connector sends it as `Authorization: Token token=<your-token>` on requests to the PagerDuty REST API.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional sync settings
setupSteps.body: Optionally set `cutoff_days` to bound how far back to read, `page_size` to control the number of records fetched per API page, and `max_retries` for retry behavior. Use `exclude_services` to skip specific services, `default_severity` for incidents lacking a severity, and the `service_details` and `incident_log_entries_overview` options to control how much detail is pulled. All of these fields are optional and have defaults if omitted.

setupSteps.title: Test the connection
setupSteps.body: Run the connection check. The connector calls the PagerDuty REST API with your token; a successful authenticated response confirms the token and configuration are valid. If you get an authentication error, verify the header format is `Token token=` and that the key has not been disabled or deleted.

sources.title: API Access Keys - PagerDuty Knowledge Base
